messages.content:
The VBA code below should check the sectorRanges value in D1 of the Active sheet and find the the sectorRanges value match in row B31:V31 in sheet 'Sector Budget' - This is the firstMatch.
Where the the sectorRanges value firstMatch is found in a column of the row B31:V31 in sheet 'Sector Budget', it the should then find within that column from row 32 to row 42 the specific subsectorRanges value that was entered in the range D6:D305 of the Active sheet - This is the secondMatch.
If the secondMatch is not found then it should pop up the appropraite message.
If the secondMatch is found then it should pop up the appropraite message and do Target.Offset(0, 1).Select.
Unfortunately, the code is always popin up the Not Found message even when matching values exist.

Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim sectorRanges As Range
    Dim subsectorRanges As Range
    Dim subsectorCells As Range
    Dim valueCells As Range
    Dim firstMatch As Range
    Dim secondMatch As Range
    
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sector Budget")
    
    'Check if the changed range is D6:D305 in the active sheet
    If Not Intersect(Target, Me.Range("D6:D305")) Is Nothing Then
        'Check if D1 has a value
        If Me.Range("D1").Value <> "" Then
            Set sectorRanges = ws.Range("B31:V31")
            Set subsectorRanges = ws.Range("B32:V42")
            
            'Find the first matching value in the row B31:V31 of sheet 'Sector Budget'
            Set firstMatch = sectorRanges.Find(What:=Me.Range("D1").Value, LookIn:=xlValues, LookAt:=xlWhole)
            
            'If a first match is found
            If Not firstMatch Is Nothing Then
                Set valueCells = subsectorRanges.Cells(firstMatch.Column - subsectorRanges.Column + 1)
                
                'Check if the value exists in the corresponding column of the range B32:V42
                Set secondMatch = valueCells.Find(What:=Target.Value, LookIn:=xlValues, LookAt:=xlWhole)
                
                If secondMatch Is Nothing Then
                    'If second match does not exist, show message and clear cell entry
                    MsgBox "The Subsector description is not correct"
                    Application.EnableEvents = False
                    Target.ClearContents
                    Application.EnableEvents = True
                Else
                    'If second match is found, show message and select Target.Offset(0, 1)
                    MsgBox "A negative value will decrease the budget." & vbCrLf & "" & vbCrLf & _
                    "A positive value will increase the budget." & vbCrLf & "" & vbCrLf & _
                    "Enter value in column E"
                    Target.Offset(0, 1).Select
                End If
            End If
        End If
    End If

There are a few potential issues with the code that could be causing it to always display the "Not Found" message:

1. Make sure the worksheet name is correct: Double-check that the name of the sheet you're trying to reference is indeed "Sector Budget". If it's not, update the code to reflect the correct sheet name.

2. Check the range references: Verify that the range references for sectorRanges (B31:V31) and subsectorRanges (B32:V42) are correct. Ensure that these ranges encompass all the necessary cells.

3. Confirm the values in D1 and D6:D305: Check the values in D1 and D6:D305 of the active sheet. Make sure they match the values in the sectorRanges and subsectorRanges ranges in the "Sector Budget" sheet. Keep in mind that the search is case-sensitive, so the values should be an exact match.

4. Check the value search criteria: Ensure that the search criteria in the firstMatch and secondMatch Find methods match the data type and format of the values in the ranges being searched. If the values being searched are numbers and not text, consider using the LookAt:=xlWhole option to search for exact matches.

5. Verify the placement of the code: Make sure the code is placed in the appropriate event handler (e.g., Worksheet_Change) and that the event handler is properly set up to trigger when changes occur in the desired range (D6:D305).

By addressing these potential issues, you should be able to resolve the problem of the code always displaying the "Not Found" message.
All the points you raised are OK. Is there a way to add a check in the code to confirm if the firstMach has been accurately found such as identifying the cell address.

Yes, you can add a check to confirm if the firstMatch has been accurately found by displaying the cell address. You can modify the code as follows:

```
If Not firstMatch Is Nothing Then
    ' Add this line to display the cell address of the firstMatch
    MsgBox "First Match Found in Cell: " & firstMatch.Address
    
    Set valueCells = subsectorRanges.Cells(firstMatch.Column - subsectorRanges.Column + 1)
    
    ' Rest of your code...
```

With this modification, when a first match is found, a message box will pop up displaying the cell address of the first match. This can help you confirm if the firstMatch is accurate and ensure that the code is searching in the expected range.

Additionally, you can also add a check for the secondMatch by displaying its cell address in a similar manner:

```
If secondMatch Is Nothing Then
    ' If second match does not exist, show message and clear cell entry
    MsgBox "The Subsector description is not correct"
    Application.EnableEvents = False
    Target.ClearContents
    Application.EnableEvents = True
Else
    ' Add this line to display the cell address of the secondMatch
    MsgBox "Second Match Found in Cell: " & secondMatch.Address
    
    ' Rest of your code...
```

By adding these checks, you can verify both the firstMatch and secondMatch cell addresses, helping to identify any potential issues with the search.

The checks you provided showed that the firstMatch was accurately found. When in the column of the found firstMatch, the code should then chack downwards from row 32 to row 42 for the secondMatch. It appears this is where it is not finding the match of the value entered in the Active sheet

If the code is finding the correct firstMatch in the column of the found value in D1, but not finding the secondMatch when searching downwards from row 32 to row 42, then there may be an issue with the range or values being searched.

To troubleshoot this issue, you can add additional checks to see if the correct range is being searched and if the secondMatch values are being evaluated correctly. Modify the code as follows:

```vba
If Not firstMatch Is Nothing Then
    MsgBox "First Match Found in Cell: " & firstMatch.Address
    
    Set valueCells = subsectorRanges.Cells(firstMatch.Column - subsectorRanges.Column + 1)
    
    ' Add these lines to display the range being searched for the secondMatch
    MsgBox "Searching in Range: " & valueCells.Address
    
    ' Check if the value exists in the corresponding column of the range B32:V42
    Set secondMatch = valueCells.Find(What:=Target.Value, LookIn:=xlValues, LookAt:=xlWhole)
    
    ' Add these lines to verify the secondMatch values being evaluated
    If Not secondMatch Is Nothing Then
        MsgBox "Second Match Found in Cell: " & secondMatch.Address
    Else
        MsgBox "Second Match Not Found"
    End If
    
    ' Rest of your code...
```

With these modifications, you will receive additional message boxes displaying the range being searched for the secondMatch and whether the secondMatch is found or not. This can help you identify if the correct range is being searched and if the values in the range match the value you are searching for.

By analyzing the displayed information, you can determine if there are any discrepancies in the range being searched or if there may be issues with the values in the secondMatch range.
It appears that the code is only searching for the secondMatch in row 32. It should search from row 32 to row 42 in the same column where the first match is found

Apologies for the oversight. To ensure that the secondMatch is found within the entire column from row 32 to row 42, you'll need to update the code to specify the correct range for the valueCells within the secondMatch search. Please try the following revised code:

```vba
If Not firstMatch Is Nothing Then
    MsgBox "First Match Found in Cell: " & firstMatch.Address
    
    Set valueCells = subsectorRanges.Columns(firstMatch.Column - subsectorRanges.Column + 1).Resize(11) ' Resize the range to include rows 32 to 42
    
    MsgBox "Searching in Range: " & valueCells.Address
    
    ' Check if the value exists in the corresponding column of the range B32:V42
    Set secondMatch = valueCells.Find(What:=Target.Value, LookIn:=xlValues, LookAt:=xlWhole)
    
    If Not secondMatch Is Nothing Then
        MsgBox "Second Match Found in Cell: " & secondMatch.Address
    Else
        MsgBox "Second Match Not Found"
    End If
    
    ' Rest of your code...
```

In the code above, we use the `Columns(...)` method to select the entire column within the range, and then use `Resize(11)` to include rows 32 to 42. This ensures that the search for the secondMatch is performed within the correct range.

Please give this revised code a try and let me know if it resolves the issue.
Applying your checks, the firstMatch was found and the secondMatch was also found. However the initial error still exists in the rest of the code, as it has not been changed

Apologies for the confusion. To address the issue in the rest of the code, please ensure that you properly handle the selection of the `Target` cell and adjust the code accordingly. 

Here's an updated version of the code to incorporate the necessary adjustments:

```vba
If Not firstMatch Is Nothing Then
    MsgBox "First Match Found in Cell: " & firstMatch.Address
    
    Set valueCells = subsectorRanges.Columns(firstMatch.Column - subsectorRanges.Column + 1).Resize(11) ' Resize the range to include rows 32 to 42
    
    MsgBox "Searching in Range: " & valueCells.Address
    
    ' Check if the value exists in the corresponding column of the range B32:V42
    Set secondMatch = valueCells.Find(What:=Target.Value, LookIn:=xlValues, LookAt:=xlWhole)
    
    If Not secondMatch Is Nothing Then
        MsgBox "Second Match Found in Cell: " & secondMatch.Address
        ' Add code here to handle when the secondMatch is found
        MsgBox "A negative value will decrease the budget." & vbCrLf & "" & vbCrLf & _
                    "A positive value will increase the budget." & vbCrLf & "" & vbCrLf & _
                    "Enter value in column E"
        Target.Offset(0, 1).Select
    Else
        MsgBox "Second Match Not Found"
        ' Add code here to handle when the secondMatch is not found
        Application.EnableEvents = False
        Target.ClearContents
        Application.EnableEvents = True
    End If
End If
```

In this updated code, after finding the `secondMatch`, it will display a message indicating that the match is found and then select the cell one column to the right of `Target` using `Target.Offset(0, 1).Select`.

Please replace the corresponding section of your original code with this updated section and test it again.

If the issue persists, please provide more details about the rest of your code for further assistance.
You're welcome! I'm glad to hear that the updated code works brilliantly for you. If you have any more questions or need further assistance, feel free to ask. Happy coding!

In this event, when I double click in column A, it copies the values in B:U to AA:AT
Is there a way of writing the code so that only the values are copied accross


Private Sub Worksheet_BeforeDoubleClick(ByVal Target As Range, Cancel As Boolean)
    Dim doubleClickedCell As Range
    
    ' Check if the cell clicked is in column A
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        doubleClickFlag = True
        Set doubleClickedCell = Target.Cells(1)
        If Target.CountLarge > 1 Then Exit Sub
        If doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 1).Value = 0 Or doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 1).Value = "" Then Exit Sub
        If doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 26).Value <> "" Then
            MsgBox "Budget has already been created. To change, clear the value in AA"
            Exit Sub
        End If
        
        Application.EnableEvents = False
        
        ' Get the range of cells to copy (B:U)
        Dim sourceRange As Range
        Set sourceRange = doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 1).Resize(1, 25)

        ' Get the range to paste (AA:AT)
        Dim destinationRange As Range
        Set destinationRange = doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 26)
        
        ' Copy the cell values
        sourceRange.Copy destinationRange
        
        MsgBox "Budget value is now available to associated Contractor page"
        
        Application.EnableEvents = True
        doubleClickFlag = False
    End If
End Sub

To copy only the values from range B:U to range AA:AT when double-clicking in column A, you can modify the code by using the `.Value` property of the range to copy and paste only the values, instead of copying the entire range object.

Here's the updated code:

```vba
Private Sub Worksheet_BeforeDoubleClick(ByVal Target As Range, Cancel As Boolean)
    Dim doubleClickedCell As Range
    
    ' Check if the cell clicked is in column A
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        doubleClickFlag = True
        Set doubleClickedCell = Target.Cells(1)
        If Target.CountLarge > 1 Then Exit Sub
        If doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 1).Value = 0 Or doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 1).Value = "" Then Exit Sub
        If doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 26).Value <> "" Then
            MsgBox "Budget has already been created. To change, clear the value in AA"
            Exit Sub
        End If
        
        Application.EnableEvents = False
        
        ' Get the range of cells to copy (B:U)
        Dim sourceRange As Range
        Set sourceRange = doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 1).Resize(1, 25)
        
        ' Get the range to paste (AA:AT)
        Dim destinationRange As Range
        Set destinationRange = doubleClickedCell.Offset(0, 26)
        
        ' Copy only the values from sourceRange to destinationRange
        destinationRange.Value = sourceRange.Value
        
        MsgBox "Budget value is now available to associated Contractor page"
        
        Application.EnableEvents = True
        doubleClickFlag = False
    End If
End Sub
```

By assigning the `.Value` property of the range, the code now copies only the values from sourceRange to destinationRange.

Give this updated code a try and let me know if it works as expected for your scenario.

In folder 'SERVICE PROVIDERS', I have a workbook named 'Service Providers' and within this workbook, I have a sheet named 'Sector Budget'.
In the folder 'SERVICE PROVIDERS' I have a folder named 'z BudgetDetails' which has a workbook named 'BudgetDetails'.
In the workbook 'BudgetDetails' I have a worksheet named 'SectorBudget'.

I want the worksheet 'SectorBudget' in workbook 'BudgetDetails' to have a value and format only copy of the worksheet 'Sector Budget' in workbook 'Service Providers'.

When both workbooks are open, I want the worksheet 'Sector Budget' in workbook 'Service Providers' to be copied values and format only (without formulas) to the worksheet 'SectorBudget' in workbook 'BudgetDetails'.

If the workbook 'BudgetDetails' is open and the workbook 'Service Providers' is not open, the attempt to copy should be aborted.

Is there a way to do this.

Yes, it is possible to achieve the desired copying of values and formatting from the worksheet 'Sector Budget' in the 'Service Providers' workbook to the 'SectorBudget' worksheet in the 'BudgetDetails' workbook. You can use VBA code to automate this process. 

Here's an example of how you can accomplish this:

```vba
Sub CopySectorBudget()
    Dim srcWorkbook As Workbook
    Dim destWorkbook As Workbook
    Dim srcWorksheet As Worksheet
    Dim destWorksheet As Worksheet
    
    ' Check if the 'Service Providers' workbook is open
    On Error Resume Next
    Set srcWorkbook = Workbooks("Service Providers.xlsx")
    On Error GoTo 0
    
    If srcWorkbook Is Nothing Then
        MsgBox "The 'Service Providers' workbook is not open. Aborting copy."
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    Set srcWorksheet = srcWorkbook.Sheets("Sector Budget")
    
    ' Check if the 'BudgetDetails' workbook is open
    On Error Resume Next
    Set destWorkbook = Workbooks("BudgetDetails.xlsx")
    On Error GoTo 0
    
    If destWorkbook Is Nothing Then
        MsgBox "The 'BudgetDetails' workbook is not open. Aborting copy."
        Exit Sub
    End If
    
    Set destWorksheet = destWorkbook.Sheets("SectorBudget")
    
    ' Copy values and formatting from the source worksheet to the destination worksheet
    srcWorksheet.UsedRange.Copy
    destWorksheet.Range("A1").PasteSpecial xlPasteValues
    destWorksheet.Range("A1").PasteSpecial xlPasteFormats
    
    Application.CutCopyMode = False
    
    MsgBox "Values and formatting have been copied from 'Sector Budget' to 'SectorBudget'."
End Sub
```

Make sure to adjust the workbook and worksheet names in the code to match your specific scenario. Additionally, ensure that both workbooks are open before running the code.

You can run this VBA code by either pressing F5 or executing it from a button or another event trigger in your 'BudgetDetails' workbook.

This code checks if both workbooks are open and then copies the values and formatting from the 'Sector Budget' worksheet in the 'Service Providers' workbook to the 'SectorBudget' worksheet in the 'BudgetDetails' workbook. It also displays a message to indicate that the copy operation has been completed.

Please note that this code only copies values and formatting, without formulas, as per your requirement.
Where in the workbook BudgetDetails should I place this code

To ensure that the VBA code is executed correctly, you can place it in the code module for the 'BudgetDetails' workbook. 

Here's how you can add the code to the 'BudgetDetails' workbook:

1. Open the 'BudgetDetails' workbook in Excel.
